title: lagoseris ruppelii
intro: Lagoseris ruppelii, also known as the Ruppel's desert tea or Ruppel's lagoseris, is a rare succulent plant native to the deserts of North Africa and the Middle East. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. Lagoseris ruppelii has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues, due to its health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving respiratory function. Herbal preparations of Lagoseris ruppelii typically involve infusions or decoctions of the leaves and stems, which can be consumed as teas or used in topical applications for skin conditions.
